name: TAO Post Expansion Runner v1
thesis: TAO expansion bars can keep trending if the first profit is harvested while a core runner is left for persistence. The edge should come from post-expansion continuation with active de-risking rather than all-or-nothing exits.
invalidation_condition: Abort if post-expansion logic still collapses into sparse no-trade grammar, contradictory confirmation overlap, or fails to produce post-cost edge.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Impulse expansion that can continue for a few bars if the first burst is not fully faded.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: After an expansion bar, partial profit plus retained runner can monetize both immediate impulse and occasional continuation without requiring perfect exits.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h produces large enough expansions for staged exits to matter when continuation actually appears.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If expansions fade too quickly in chop, the runner logic may not earn enough follow-through to justify entry.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If participation exists but follow-through is weak, shorten the runner and tighten time-based de-risk before changing the trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; expansion and persistence are aligned
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by moderate expansion threshold and simple hold logic
